book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.

ccel.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

source.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

title: Almighty.

source.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Jeremiah xxxix.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: 18, 19 (Septuagint).

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Great, the strong God, Lord of great Counsel, and mighty in His works, the Great God, the Lord Almighty and of great name.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: By believing In One God we cut off all misbelief in many gods, using this as a shield against Greeks; and every opposing power of heretics; and by adding, In One God the Father, we contend against those of the circumcision, who deny the Only-begotten Son of God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For, as was said yesterday, even before explaining the truths concerning our Lord Jesus Christ, we made it manifest at once, by saying “The Father,” that He is the Father of a Son: that as we understand that God is, so we may understand that He has a Son.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But to those titles we add that He is also “Almighty;” and this we affirm because of Greeks and Jews together, and all heretics.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For of the Greeks some have said that God is the soul of the world: and others that His power reaches only to heaven, and not to earth as well.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Some also sharing their error and misusing the text which says, “And Thy truth unto the clouds,” have dared to circumscribe God’s providence by the clouds and the heaven, and to alienate from God the things on earth; having forgotten the Psalm which says, If I go up into heaven, Thou art there, if I go down into hell, Thou art present.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For if there is nothing higher than heaven, and if hell is deeper than the earth, He who rules the lower regions reaches the earth also.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But heretics again, as I have said before, know not One Almighty God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For He is Almighty who rules all things, who has power over all things.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But they who say that one God is Lord of the soul, and some other of the body, make neither of them perfect, because either is wanting to the other.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For how is he almighty, who has power over the soul, but not over the body?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And how is he almighty who has dominion over bodies, but no power over spirits?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But these men the Lord confutes, saying on the contrary, Rather fear ye Him which is able to destroy both soul and body in hell.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For unless the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ has the power over both, how does He subject both to punishment?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For how shall He be able to take the body which is another’s and cast it into hell, except He first bind the strong man, and spoil his goods?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But the Divine Scripture and the doctrines of the truth know but One God, who rules all things by His power, but endures many things of His will.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For He rules even over the idolaters, but endures them of His forbearance: He rules also over the heretics who set Him at nought, but bears with them because of His long-suffering: He rules even over the devil, but bears with him of His long-suffering, not from want of power; as if defeated.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For he is the beginning of the Lord’s creation, made to be mocked, not by Himself, for that were unworthy of Him, but by the Angels whom He hath made.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But He suffered him to live, for two purposes, that he might disgrace himself the more in his defeat, and that mankind might be crowned with victory.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: O all wise providence of God! which takes the wicked purpose for a groundwork of salvation for the faithful.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For as He took the unbrotherly purpose of Joseph’s brethren for a groundwork of His own dispensation, and, by permitting them to sell their brother from hatred, took occasion to make him king whom He would; so he permitted the devil to wrestle, that the victors might be crowned; and that when victory was gained, he might be the more disgraced as being conquered by the weaker, and men be greatly honoured as having conquered him who was once an Archangel.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Nothing then is withdrawn from the power of God; for the Scripture says of Him, for all things are Thy servants.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: All things alike are His servants, but from all these One, His only Son, and One, His Holy Spirit, are excepted; and all the things which are His servants serve the Lord through the One Son and in the Holy Spirit.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: God then rules all, and of His long-suffering endures even murderers and robbers and fornicators, having appointed a set time for recompensing every one, that if they who have had long warning are still impenitent in heart, they may receive the greater condemnation.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: They are kings of men, who reign upon earth, but not without the power from above: and this Nebuchadnezzar once learned by experience, when he said; For His kingdom is an everlasting kingdom, and His power from generation to generation.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Riches, and gold, and silver are not, as some think, the devil’s: for the whole world of riches is for the faithful man, but for the faithless not even a penny.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Now nothing is more faithless than the devil; and God says plainly by the Prophet, The gold is Mine, and the silver is Mine, and to whomsoever I will I give it.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Do thou but use it well, and there is no fault to be found with money: but whenever thou hast made a bad use of that which is good, then being unwilling to blame thine own management, thou impiously throwest back the blame upon the Creator.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: A man may even be justified by money: I was hungry, and ye gave Me meat: that certainly was from money.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I was naked, and ye clothed Me: that certainly was by money.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And wouldest thou learn that money may become a door of the kingdom of heaven?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Sell, saith He, that thou hast, and give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Now I have made these remarks because of those heretics who count possessions, and money, and men’s bodies accursed.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For I neither wish thee to be a slave of money, nor to treat as enemies the things which God has given thee for use.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Never say then that riches are the devil’s: for though he say, All these will I give thee, for they are delivered unto me, one may indeed even reject his assertion; for we need not believe the liar: and yet perhaps he spake the truth, being compelled by the power of His presence: for he said not, All these will I give thee, for they are mine, but, for they are delivered unto me.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He grasped not the dominion of them, but confessed that he had been entrusted with them, and was for a time dispensing them.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But at a proper time interpreters should inquire whether his statement is false or true.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: God then is One, the Father, the Almighty, whom the brood of heretics have dared to blaspheme.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Yea, they have dared to blaspheme the Lord of Sabaoth, who sitteth above the Cherubim: they have dared to blaspheme the Lord Adonai: they have dared to blaspheme Him who is in the Prophets the Almighty God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But worship thou One God the Almighty, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Flee from the error of many gods, flee also from every heresy, and say like Job, But I will call upon the Almighty Lord, which doeth great things and unsearchable, glorious things and marvellous without number, and, For all these things there is honour from the Almighty: to Whom be the glory for ever and ever.
